name: Dingo
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Try to defeat it quickly to avoid damage. Attack swiftly with Tidus."
location_notes: You can find them along any of the pathways on Besaid Island.

name: Condor
sensor_text: "Aerial. Magic attacks are the way to go."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. Use Wakka's attack to knock it down. It's vulnerable to fire, so you can also use Lulu's Fire spell."
location_notes: You can find them along any of the pathways on Besaid Island.

name: Water Flan
sensor_text: "Water elemental. Vulnerable to opposing element. Resistant to physical attacks."
scan_text: "Water-based. So soft, it's hard to damage physically. Weak against lightning, so blast it with Lulu's Thunder spell. Don't use Water or it will recover HP."
location_notes: You can find them along any of the pathways on Besaid Island.

name: Dinonix
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Defeat it quickly to avoid damage. Vulnerable to ice."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Kilika Woods.

name: Killer Bee
sensor_text: "Aerial. Vulnerable to ice. Watch out for poison."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. Use Wakka's attack to knock it down. It's vulnerable to ice, so Blizzard is also effective. Attacks may inflict poison."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Kilika Woods.

name: Yellow Element
scan_text: "Casts Thunder. Physical attacks hardly hurt it. Absorbs lightning, so Water is the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Kilika Woods.

name: Ragora
scan_text: "Watch out for its powerful Seed Cannon. One bad hit will knock characters out. Plants tend to be weak against fire, so have Lulu cast Fira."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Kilika Woods.

name: Raptor
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Defeat it quickly to avoid damage. Takes only half damage from fire and lightning, but vulnerable to ice."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road or the Djose Highroad.

name: Gandarewa
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. Casts Thunder. It's vulnerable to water, but has high magic defense. Use Wakka to knock it out of the sky."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road, the Djose Highroad, or the road to the Moonflow.

name: Thunder Flan
sensor_text: "Lightning elemental. Vulnerable to opposing element. Resistant to physical attacks."
scan_text: "Lightning-based. So soft, it's hard to damage physically. Weak against water, so blast it with Water. Don't use lightning, or it will recover HP."
location_notes: You can find them along the Mushroom Rock Road or in the latter portions of the Mi'ihen Highroad.

name: Red Element
scan_text: "Casts Fire. Physical attacks hardly hurt it. Absorbs fire, so Blizzard is the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road.

name: Lamashtu
sensor_text: "Covered in hard scales. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for Slow."
scan_text: "Covered with hard scales. Physical and magic defense are high, but it's weak against fire. Its attack may cause slow. Watch out for its ice-based Breath, which hits all."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road or the Djose Highroad.

name: Funguar
sensor_text: "Occasionally counters with Pollen, which induces sleep."
scan_text: "Casts Fire, so it's best to silence it. Occasionally retaliates with sleep-inducing Pollen. Putting it to sleep is effective. Vulnerable to fire."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road.

name: Garuda
sensor_text: "Dodging its attacks is easier after inflicting darkness."
scan_text: "It has high strength and tons of HP. Beware of Sonic Boom, which hits all characters. Dodge its attacks more easily by inflicting darkness."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mushroom Rock Road. They are easier to find along the latter sections of the pathway, especially on the elevated portions of the road near the elevators. Many players report success hunting for them in the area where Luzzu and Gatta were standing.

name: Garm
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Defeat it quickly to avoid damage. It's vulnerable to lightning and fire."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad.

name: Simurgh
scan_text: "Aerial. Magic attacks are the way to go. Watch out for darkness."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad.

name: Bite Bug
scan_text: "Aerial. Vulnerable to ice. Watch out for poison."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad.

name: Snow Flan
sensor_text: "Ice elemental. Vulnerable to opposing element. Resistant to physical attacks."
scan_text: "Ice-based. So soft, it's hard to damage physically. Weak against fire, but will absorb HP from ice. Casts Blizzard."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad and near Lake Macalania as well.

name: Bunyip
sensor_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go."
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Slow-moving, but has high physical and magic defense. Vulnerable to ice and water. Falls easily to piercing weapons."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad.

name: Basilisk
sensor_text: "Beware the third eye, its Stone Gaze may cause petrification. If petrified, restore with Esuna or Soft."
scan_text: "Beware the third eye. Its Stone Gaze may cause petrification. If petrified, restore with Esuna or Soft. Kimahri can learn Stone Breath."
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad.

name: Ochu
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them all along the Djose Highroad, but you can also find them along the Moonflow. In fact, they are much easier to find along the Moonflow, so head to that pathway if you need to capture them.

name: Melusine
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Thunder Plains. They tend to be more abundant in the first section of the Thunder Plains.

name: Aerouge
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. It's weak against water, but has high magic defense. The only attack it has is Thundara, so inflict silence to leave it powerless."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Thunder Plains.

name: Buer
scan_text: "Aerial. Resistant to magic. Watch out for confusion."
location_notes: You can find them in the Thunder Plains.

name: Gold Element
scan_text: "Casts Thunder. Physical attacks hardly hurt it. Absorbs lightning, so use Watera for an Overkill."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Thunder Plains, but they are more common in the first portion.

name: Kusariqqu
scan_text: "Covered in hard scales. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for Slow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Thunder Plains.

name: Larva
sensor_text: "Casts Thundara on itself to replenish HP and grow even more powerful."
scan_text: "When HP is halved, it casts Thundara on itself to recover HP and increase magic power. Silence it with Silence Attack. It's vulnerable to water, so Watera is the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Thunder Plains.

name: Iron Giant
sensor_text: "Dark Buster is effective, but beware its Reaper attack."
scan_text: "Targets character with lowest HP. Beware its Reaper attack, which hits all characters. No elemental weaknesses. Takes half damage from lightning. Use piercing weapons and Dark Buster."
location_notes: You can find them in the second half of the Thunder Plains.

name: Qactuar
sensor_text: "You'll get a treat if you can defeat it before it runs away."
scan_text: "Its 1000 Needles inflict 1000 points of damage. Resistant to both physical and magical attacks. Runs away when HP is low. Threaten is recommended."
location_notes: You can find them in the Thunder Plains, but they need to be unlocked. Check out theSpirit Lancepage in the Celestial Weapons section for more details.

name: Snow Wolf
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Defeat it quickly to avoid damage. It's vulnerable to fire."
location_notes: You can find them near Lake Macalania.

name: Iguion
scan_text: Petrify
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in Macalania Woods.

name: Wasp
sensor_text: "Aerial. Vulnerable to ice. Watch out for poison."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. Use Wakka's attack to knock it down. It's vulnerable to ice, so cast Blizzara to achieve Overkill. Attacks may inflict poison."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Macalania Woods.

name: Evil Eye
sensor_text: "Aerial. Resistant to magic. Watch out for confusion."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. Its weakness is fire, but it has high magic defense. Its Gaze may cause confusion."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Macalania Woods.

name: Ice Flan
sensor_text: "Ice elemental. Vulnerable to opposing element. Resistant to physical attacks."
scan_text: "Ice-based. So soft, it's hard to damage physically. Weak against fire, but will absorb HP from ice."
location_notes: You can find them around Lake Macalania.

name: Blue Element
scan_text: "Casts Watera. Physical attacks hardly hurt it. Absorbs water, so Thundara is the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Macalania Woods.

name: Murussu
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for slow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in Macalania Woods.

name: Mafdet
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for slow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere near Lake Macalania.

name: Xiphos
scan_text: "Occasionally succumbs to darkness."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Macalania Woods.

name: Chimera
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Macalania Woods.

name: Sand Wolf
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island.

name: Alcyone
scan_text: "Aerial. Magic attacks are the way to go. Watch out for Darkness."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island.

name: Mushussu
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for Slow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island. Your best bet is to head further north into the Island, though, to see more of them (towards Home).

name: Zu
scan_text: "Watch out for Sonic Boom when wings glow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island, but the best place to find them is to head back to the sign that says "weak enemies to the right, strong enemies to the left" (in Al Bhed) and head to the left.

name: Sand Worm
scan_text: Poison
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island, but the best place to find them is to head back to the sign that says "weak enemies to the right, strong enemies to the left" (in Al Bhed) and head to the left.

name: Cactuar
sensor_text: "You'll get a treat if you can defeat it before it runs away."
scan_text: "Its 10000 Needles inflict 10000 points of damage. Resistant to both physical and magical attacks. Runs away when HP is low. Hard to defeat, but worth a lot of AP."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere on Bikanel Island, but the easiest place to find them is near theVillage of the Cactuars.

name: Skoll
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them all over the Calm Lands. They are one of the easiest fiends to find in this area.

name: Nebiros
scan_text: "Aerial. Vulnerable to Ice. Watch out for Poison."
location_notes: You can find them all over the Calm Lands. They're probably the easiest fiend to track down in this area.

name: Flame Flan
sensor_text: "Fire elemental. Vulnerable to opposing element. Resistant to physical attacks."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Calm Lands.

name: Shred
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for Slow."
location_notes: You can find them over the Calm Lands. They are one of the easiest fiends to track down in this area.

name: Anacondaur
scan_text: "Watch out for its Stone Gaze."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Calm Lands, but they are most abundant in the northwest section.

name: Ogre
sensor_text: "Be careful if it puts up its dukes."
scan_text: "When it puts up its dukes, it will respond to physical attacks with a mighty counterattack. Fire-based attacks are the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them in a few areas of the Calm Lands, but they tend to be easiest to track down in the northwestern and northeastern sections of the map.

name: Coeurl
sensor_text: "Casts Drain and Blaster when low on HP."
scan_text: "High magic attack and defense. Casts Thundara and Blizzara. Also casts Drain when HP is low. Beware of Blaster, since it's unblockable and causes petrification."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Calm Lands, but they are most abundant on the western/left side of the map.

name: Chimera Brain
sensor_text: "Darkness and silence work well. Vulnerable to Threaten."
scan_text: "The snake casts Thundara, the bull Assaults, the hawk uses Aqua Breath, and the lion uses Megiddo Flame, in that order. Vulnerable to Threaten, darkness, and silence. Kimahri can learn Aqua Breath."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Calm Lands.

name: Malboro
scan_text: Darkness
location_notes: You can find them in most places around the Calm Lands, but they are most abundant in the northwest portion of the map and the areas near where Belgemine is standing. Malboros have a very low encounter rate, so be patient and keep trying.

name: Bandersnatch
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mt. Gagazet mountain trail. This trail is the pathway that leads up to the caves you travel along after you meet with the Ronso.

name: Ahriman
sensor_text: "Aerial. Resistant to magic. Watch out for confusion."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. It has high magic defense and no elemental weaknesses. Its Gaze may cause confusion. It also has Ultrasonics, which strikes all characters."
location_notes: You can find them in the Mt. Gagazet caves. You can also find them along the Zanarkand Ruins pathway.

name: Dark Flan
sensor_text: "No elemental weaknesses. Resistant to physical attacks."
scan_text: "Resistant to physical attacks and cuts all elemental damage by half. Casts non-elemental magic like Demi, Bio, Flare, Osmose, and Drain. Kimahri can learn White Wind."
location_notes: You can find them inside the Mt. Gagazet caves, and you can also find them along the pathway to the Zanarkand Ruins. Unfortunately, it doesn't matter which section of the cave you are in; there is no way to improve your odds of finding one. Just keep hunting until you find them.

name: Grenade
sensor_text: "Retaliates with Self-Destruct when attacked three times."
scan_text: "Retaliates with Self-Destruct against whoever attacks it a third time. Weak against ice, but has high magic defense, so use Mental Break and your best ice magic. Kimahri can learn Self-Destruct."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mt. Gagazet mountain trails.

name: Grat
scan_text: "Its Seed Cannon packs quite a punch. Seed Burst doesn't do much damage, but may inflict confusion on characters. Vulnerable to fire. Kimahri can learn Seed Cannon."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Mt. Gagazet caves.

name: Grendel
sensor_text: "Inflict darkness to evade its attacks. Piercing weapons are the way to go."
scan_text: "Inflict darkness to hinder its attacks. Its Flame Ball is a powerful fire attack that hits all, but it needs to Charge before it's released. Use poison and piercing weapons. Kimahri can learn Fire Breath."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Mt. Gagazet caves or along the walkway to the Zanarkand Ruins.

name: Bashura
sensor_text: "When it puts up its dukes it will respond to physical attacks with a mighty counter. Use magic and poison to soften it up."
scan_text: "Be careful if it puts up its dukes. When it puts up its dukes, it will respond to physical attacks with a mighty counter. Use magic and poison to soften it up."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mt. Gagazet mountain trail. This trail is the pathway that leads up to the caves you travel along after meeting the Ronso.

name: Mandragora
scan_text: Darkness
location_notes: You can find Mandragora enemies within the Mt. Gagazet Caverns, and they tend to appear more commonly near the entrances and exits to the cave.

name: Behemoth
scan_text: "Its Thundara, Heave, and claw attacks are deadly. One good hit will knock you out. Kimahri can learn Mighty Guard."
location_notes: You can find them inside the Mt. Gagazet cave. They can also be found along the trail leading to the Zanarkand Ruins. Try rotating between the two areas if you're having trouble finding them until you get lucky.

name: Splasher
scan_text: "When defeated, it sometimes inflicts damage on its attacker with Self-Destruct."
location_notes: You can find them in the underwater sections of the Mt. Gagazet Cave exclusively. Find the part of the cave where your party members are required to swim, and you will encounter them regularly.

name: Achelous
sensor_text: "Watch out when it starts glowing. It releases Sonic Wave after the third pulse."
scan_text: "It starts glowing when hit. After the 3rdpulse, it attacks with a Sonic Wave. Put it to sleep to prevent it from glowing. It takes no damage from water, but is weak against lightning."
location_notes: You can only find them in the underwater sections of the Mt. Gagazet Cave exclusively. Find the part of the cave where your party members are required to swim, and you will encounter them regularly.

name: Maelspike
scan_text: "Watch out for Maelstrom."
location_notes: You can only find them in the underwater sections of the Mt. Gagazet Cave exclusively. Find the part of the cave where your party members are required to swim, and you will encounter them regularly.

name: Exoray
scan_text: Poison
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin.

name: Wraith
scan_text: Immune to Sensor
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin or in the Omega Ruins.

name: Gemini (Sword)
sensor_text: "Together they unleash Double Reaper. Focus your attacks to defeat one quickly."
scan_text: "After receiving a certain amount of damage, they unleash a powerful synchronized attack, the Double Reaper. One absorbs lightning and fire, while the other absorbs water and ice."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin. There is also one set that you will battle in theOmega Ruins.

name: Gemini (Club)
sensor_text: "Together they unleash Double Reaper. Focus your attacks to defeat one quickly."
scan_text: "After receiving a certain amount of damage, they unleash a powerful synchronized attack, the Double Reaper. One absorbs lightning and fire, while the other absorbs water and ice."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin. There is also one set that you will battle in theOmega Ruins.

name: Demonolith
scan_text: Darkness
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin and in Omega Ruins.

name: Great Malboro
sensor_text: "Breathes Bad Breath1⁄3of the time. Be ready for anything."
scan_text: "Danger! There is a 1/3 chance of it attacking with Bad Breath. To avoid being wiped out, equip armor that guards against status effects and attack with fire. Kimahri can learn Bad Breath."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin or in the Omega Ruins.

name: Barbatos
sensor_text: "Covered in thick armor. Armor Break works well, but watch out for a powerful counterattack. All of its attacks deal great damage, so beware."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin.

name: Adamantoise
scan_text: "Protected by a hard shell. Attacks multiple targets with its fire-based Breath. Armor Break and Mental Break are the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin but also in the Omega Ruins. They are one of the more common enemies that you will find in these areas.

name: Behemoth King
scan_text: "Its claw, lightning, and Heave attacks are deadly. Watch out for the Meteor it casts when it dies. Cast Protect or defend with the (triangle) button to survive. Kimahri can learn Mighty Guard."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere Inside Sin. They tend to be more abundant the closer you get to the end boss.

name: Malboro Menace
scan_text: Darkness

name: Coeurlregina
scan_text: Curse

name: Jormungand
scan_text: Immune

name: Don Tonberry
sensor_text: Damage = (Total Number of Enemies Defeated by Target) * 100
scan_text: Immune

name: Abaddon
scan_text: Darkness

name: Fenrir
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Ornitholestes
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Pteryx
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Hornet
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Vidatu
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: One-Eye
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Jumbo Flan
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Nega Elemental
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Tanket
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Fafnir
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Sleep Sprout
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Bomb King
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Juggernaut
scan_text: "Immune to sensors."

name: Ironclad
scan_text: "Immune to sensors."

name: Earth Eater
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Greater Sphere
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Catastrophe
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Th'uban
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Neslug
scan_text: Mental Break

name: Ultima Buster
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Shinryu
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Nemesis
scan_text: "Immune to scans."

name: Yowie
sensor_text: "Very nimble. Resistant to magic. Watch out for petrification and silence."
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Takes only half damage from fire, lightning and water. Attacks may cause petrification and silence."
location_notes: They can be found anywhere in the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth. They tend to be more abundant near and around the Save Sphere at the entrance.

name: Imp
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. It's weak against water, but has high magic defense. The only attack it has is Thundaga, so inflict silence to leave it powerless."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Dark Element
sensor_text: "No elemental weaknesses. Casts Reflect on itself."
scan_text: "No elemental weaknesses, and physical attacks hardly hurt it. Casts Reflect on itself. If its Reflect status is dispelled, it retaliates with Bio, Osmose, and Drain."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Nidhogg
scan_text: "Covered with hard scales. Piercing weapons are the way to go. Watch out for Slow."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Thorn
sensor_text: "Counters physical attacks with Pollen, which induces sleep and darkness."
scan_text: "Casts Fira. Putting it to sleep is effective. Releases sleep and darkness-inducing Pollen when hit by a weapon. Vulnerable to fire."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Valaha
scan_text: "Inflict darkness to hinder its attacks. Its Flame Ball is a powerful fire attack that hits all, but it needs to Charge before it's released. Kimahri can learn Fire Breath."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth, though they are rare. Unfortunately, there are no secret spaces you can go to that make it easier to find them—just keep searching!

name: Epaaj
scan_text: "Watch out for poison."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Ghost
scan_text: "Casts Doom at the beginning of the battle."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth.

name: Tonberry
sensor_text: Damage = (Total Number of Enemies Defeated by Target) * 10
scan_text: "Advances closer four times. Defeat it before then or elseâ¦"
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Cavern of the Stolen Fayth. That said, they are very rare. Many players report successfully encountering more Tonberries in the green rooms of the cave.

name: Zaurus
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Omega Ruins. They tend to be more abundant near and around the Save Sphere at the entrance.

name: Floating Death
sensor_text: "Aerial. Resistant to magic. Watch out for confusion."
scan_text: "Aerial and hard to hit. It has high magic defense and no elemental weaknesses. Its Gaze may cause confusion. It also has Ultrasonics, which strikes all characters."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Omega Ruins. They tend to spawn most frequently in the area where you found theAl Bhed Primer (Vol. XXVI)on the ground and in the hallways surrounding it, and further beyond that.

name: Black Element
scan_text: "Immune to sensors."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Omega Ruins. They tend to be most abundant in the early sections of the Omega Ruins and not too far in. Reset your game if you are having trouble making them appear.

name: Halma
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Omega Ruins. They tend to be most abundant in the areas just north of the Save Sphere when you first enter the Omega Ruins.

name: Puroboros
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Omega Ruins. They tend to be most abundant in the hallways near and around the Save Sphere.

name: Spirit
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere in the Omega Ruins. They tend to be most abundant in the hallways near and around the Save Sphere.

name: Machea
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Omega Ruins. They tend to be most abundant in the areas just north of the Save Sphere when you first enter the Omega Ruins.

name: Master Coeurl
sensor_text: "Casts Drain,Confuseand Blaster when low on HP."
scan_text: Confuse
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Omega Ruins.

name: Master Tonberry
sensor_text: Damage = (Total Number of Enemies Defeated by Target) * 10
scan_text: "Advances closer four times. Defeat it before then or elseâ¦"
location_notes: You can find them anywhere inside the Omega Ruins. The easiest place to find them in the Ruins is to travel up to the second level, where you fought against Omega Weapon. They are much more common in that area.

name: Varuna
scan_text: "CastsHasteon himself and attacks with magic, so use Dispel on him. After his Mana Focus, he'll cast Emblem of Fate. You can reduce damage with Shell, but he can cast Dispel on you, too."
location_notes: You can find them in the Omega Ruins, but they can be challenging to track down and capture. There is no specific area where they are more abundant, but they do become more abundant in the areas around where you fight Omega Weapon after it is defeated. Refer to theOmega Ruinsguide in the Side Quests section for additional information.

name: Mi'ihen Fang
scan_text: Silence
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad.

name: Ipiria
scan_text: "Very nimble and hard to hit. Magic defense is also high. Defeat it quickly to avoid damage. Takes only half damage from fire, but vulnerable to ice."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad, but they are more prevalent in the later sections of the road, particularly along the Oldroad.

name: Floating Eye
scan_text: "Immune to scans."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad but they are most common in the earlier sections of the road.

name: White Element
scan_text: "Casts Blizzard. Physical attacks hardly hurt it. Absorbs ice, so Fire is the way to go."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad but they are most abundant in the first sections of the road near Luca.

name: Raldo
sensor_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go."
scan_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Slow-moving, but has high physical and magic defense. Vulnerable to ice. Falls easily to piercing weapons. Have Auron slice it down the middle."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad.

name: Vouivre
sensor_text: "Covered by a hard shell. Piercing weapons are the way to go."
scan_text: "Covered with hard scales. Physical and magic defense are high, but it's weak against fire. Its attack may cause slow. Watch out for its ice-based Breath, which hits all."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad but they are most bountiful down along the Oldroad pathway.

name: Bomb
sensor_text: "Retaliates with Self-Destruct when attacked three times."
scan_text: "Retaliates with Self-Destruct against whoever attacks it a third time. UseNulBlazeif you can't defeat it before then. Vulnerable to ice, so use Blizzard. Kimahri can learn Self-Destruct."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad.

name: Dual Horn
scan_text: "Its Gore is powerful, but inflict darkness or use Sleep attack to avoid it easily. Kimahri can learn Fire Breath."
location_notes: You can find them anywhere along the Mi'ihen Highroad but they are most common in the earlier sections of the road.
